title: Batch vs streaming tradeoffs
description: Batch is simple, cheap, and trivially replayable; streaming gives low latency at the cost of stateful operators, watermarks, and harder backfills.
body: Use when choosing or explaining a processing model. Batch reprocesses a bounded input and is easy to reason about and backfill, but latency is the schedule interval. Streaming processes unbounded data with low latency but introduces windowing, watermarks for late/out-of-order events, large operator state, and backfills that must replay through the same stateful logic. Do not treat streaming as batch with a smaller interval. Actions: pick batch unless freshness demands streaming. Validation signals include meeting the freshness SLO with the simpler model. Risk boundary: underestimating streaming's state and backfill complexity leads to fragile pipelines; size the operational cost first.
